description: WebAuthn / passkey bypass — most checks happen client-side or via untrusted attestation; recovery flows often weaker than the passkey itself. Bug-bounty payouts in 2024-2026 commonly exploit attestation-none acceptance, recovery-code reuse, PRF extension confusion, and fallback-to-password downgrades.
craft_guidance: 1. ENUMERATE THE FLOW: registration (POST /webauthn/register/begin + /finish), authentication (assertion), recovery (codes / email link), management (list / delete authenticators).

2. ATTESTATION TIER: the client sends an attestationObject; many servers accept attestation=none (no signing key). Test by replacing attestationObject with a self-generated 'none' fmt blob; if server accepts, attacker controls credentialPublicKey.
3. CHALLENGE NONCE: server-side challenge MUST be single-use and bound to session. Replay the challenge; reuse the assertion across sessions; check if Authenticator.signCount is validated.
4. ORIGIN VALIDATION: rpId is set client-side. Verify server enforces rpId in clientDataJSON matches request Origin. Slack misconfigs allow .example.com to authenticate as login.example.com.
5. PRF EXTENSION ABUSE: WebAuthn PRF (pseudorandom function) outputs are bound to RP. Some libs leak PRF output across RPs; confirm server-derived secrets aren't reused across services.
6. RECOVERY CODE WEAKNESS: passkey itself is strong; recovery codes are usually 6-10 digits. Test rate limit, codespace size, reuse after success, code-per-account vs global.
7. FALLBACK DOWNGRADE: 'forgot passkey' often falls back to password+SMS. Confirm fallback isn't trivially triggered by attacker (no email confirmation, no rate limit).
8. CONDITIONAL UI: navigator.credentials.get({mediation:'conditional'}) reveals which usernames have passkeys — username enumeration class.
9. AUTHENTICATOR LIST DISCLOSURE: GET /webauthn/credentials may leak credential IDs that confirm account existence even when the rest of the API doesn't.
10. TOKEN BINDING: assertion sometimes piggybacks on session cookie. Verify the cookie+assertion combination is bound; otherwise stolen cookie + valid passkey = ATO.

attestation_none_acceptance.description: Server accepts attestation format 'none' — meaning the public key is unattested. Attacker registers their own keypair, uses it to authenticate. Affects WebAuthn libs that prioritize 'attestation: preferred' over 'direct'.

probes.description: Register a passkey with fmt='none' and an attacker-controlled public key. If server accepts and stores, attacker can later authenticate.

challenge_replay.description: Server's WebAuthn challenge is meant to be single-use. Some implementations cache challenges in DB without invalidation, allowing replay.

probes.description: Replay a captured WebAuthn assertion (clientDataJSON + authenticatorData + signature) with same challenge. Server must reject; if accepted, replay is possible.
Modify authenticatorData.signCount to LOWER value than recorded. Spec requires reject; lazy implementations don't track signCount.

rp_id_origin_mismatch.description: rpId in clientDataJSON should match the request Origin. Misconfigurations on subdomain-hosted apps allow attacker-controlled subdomain to authenticate on parent.

probes.description: clientDataJSON with attacker-origin while authenticating on legit.example.com. Server must validate rpId matches the registered RP, not just eTLD+1.

recovery_code_weakness.description: Recovery codes circumvent the passkey. Common bugs: short codespace, no rate limit, reuse after success, server-side codespace exhaustion.

probes.description: Submit weak recovery code. Track rate-limit response across N attempts. If no rate limit at /recovery/verify, codespace is brute-forceable.
Replay a recovery code that was already consumed. Server should mark used; some implementations only invalidate on successful login, allowing pre-success replay.

passkey_to_password_downgrade.description: Account has passkey registered but password is still active server-side. Attacker phishes / brute-forces password and bypasses passkey entirely. Common when 'add passkey' is offered without 'remove password'.

probes.description: Attempt password login on an account that has a registered passkey. If password login succeeds without forcing passkey, downgrade is possible.

conditional_ui_user_enum.description: navigator.credentials.get({mediation:'conditional'}) shows usernames that have a registered credential. Server-side: GET /webauthn/credentials/exists?username=X often leaks via response shape.

probes.description: Probe username via /webauthn/login/begin. Server response typically contains allowCredentials list. If list is empty for invalid users vs populated for valid, enumeration confirmed.
